source_paper_title: Relaxed Equilibria for Time-Inconsistent Markov Decision Processes

Page 10, Remark 2.6 (end of Section 2.2).

Certainly, to make this method fully rigorous, it remains to show the theoretic convergence of the single-valued fixed-point iteration, which is a nontrivial problem in itself and will be left for future research.
title: Convergence of single-valued fixed-point iteration for Gibbs-form equilibrium operators
background_latex:
Consider a discrete-time infinite-horizon Markov decision process (MDP) with finite state space \(S=\{1,\dots,d\}\) and compact action space \(U\subset \mathbb{R}^\ell\) with \(0<\mathrm{Leb}(U)<\infty\). A relaxed (randomized) feedback control is a map \(\pi:S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\), where \(\mathcal{P}(U)\) denotes probability measures on \(U\). For \(\lambda>0\), an entropy-regularized objective is used in which the stage payoff includes a Shannon differential entropy term. Under entropy regularization, the equilibrium (in the sense of intra-personal subgame-perfect equilibrium for time-inconsistent discounting) can be characterized as a fixed point of a single-valued operator of Gibbs (softmax) form.

More concretely, for each state \(i\in S\) and vector \(y\in\mathbb{R}^d\), define a probability density \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)\in \mathcal{D}(U)\) by
\[\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)(u):=\frac{\exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,u)+p_i^u\cdot y)\right)}{\int_U \exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,v)+p_i^v\cdot y)\right)\,dv},\qquad u\in U,\]
where \(f(0,i,u)\) is the one-step reward (at time-difference \(0\)) and \(p_i^u\in\mathbb{R}^d\) is the transition-probability row vector from state \(i\) under action \(u\). Given \(y\), the corresponding relaxed control is \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot):S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\). Let \(J_\lambda^\pi\) be the entropy-regularized value functional and let \(V_\lambda^\pi\in\mathbb{R}^d\) be the associated continuation-value vector (expected future discounted reward plus discounted entropy starting one step ahead). Define the induced single-valued self-map \(\Psi_\lambda:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^d\) by
\[\Psi_\lambda(y):=V_\lambda^{\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)}.\]
Fixed points \(y^\star\) of \(\Psi_\lambda\) correspond to entropy-regularized relaxed equilibria \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\).

A natural numerical approach is fixed-point iteration on \(\Psi_\lambda\) (equivalently on the policy operator \(\Phi_\lambda(\pi):=\Gamma_\lambda(V_\lambda^\pi,\cdot)\)), i.e., start from some \(y_0\in\mathbb{R}^d\) (or \(\pi_0\)) and generate \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) (or \(\pi_{n+1}=\Phi_\lambda(\pi_n)\)).
problem_definition_latex:
Give conditions under which the single-valued fixed-point iteration
\[y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\qquad (n\ge 0)
\]
converges (e.g., from arbitrary initialization, or from some specified set of initial points) to a fixed point \(y^\star\in\mathbb{R}^d\) satisfying \(\Psi_\lambda(y^\star)=y^\star\), equivalently producing a regular relaxed equilibrium \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\) for the entropy-regularized time-inconsistent MDP.
latex:
Consider a discrete-time infinite-horizon Markov decision process (MDP) with finite state space \(S=\{1,\dots,d\}\) and compact action space \(U\subset \mathbb{R}^\ell\) with \(0<\mathrm{Leb}(U)<\infty\). A relaxed (randomized) feedback control is a map \(\pi:S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\), where \(\mathcal{P}(U)\) denotes probability measures on \(U\). For \(\lambda>0\), an entropy-regularized objective is used in which the stage payoff includes a Shannon differential entropy term. Under entropy regularization, the equilibrium (in the sense of intra-personal subgame-perfect equilibrium for time-inconsistent discounting) can be characterized as a fixed point of a single-valued operator of Gibbs (softmax) form.

More concretely, for each state \(i\in S\) and vector \(y\in\mathbb{R}^d\), define a probability density \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)\in \mathcal{D}(U)\) by
\[\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)(u):=\frac{\exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,u)+p_i^u\cdot y)\right)}{\int_U \exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,v)+p_i^v\cdot y)\right)\,dv},\qquad u\in U,\]
where \(f(0,i,u)\) is the one-step reward (at time-difference \(0\)) and \(p_i^u\in\mathbb{R}^d\) is the transition-probability row vector from state \(i\) under action \(u\). Given \(y\), the corresponding relaxed control is \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot):S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\). Let \(J_\lambda^\pi\) be the entropy-regularized value functional and let \(V_\lambda^\pi\in\mathbb{R}^d\) be the associated continuation-value vector (expected future discounted reward plus discounted entropy starting one step ahead). Define the induced single-valued self-map \(\Psi_\lambda:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^d\) by
\[\Psi_\lambda(y):=V_\lambda^{\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)}.\]
Fixed points \(y^\star\) of \(\Psi_\lambda\) correspond to entropy-regularized relaxed equilibria \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\).

A natural numerical approach is fixed-point iteration on \(\Psi_\lambda\) (equivalently on the policy operator \(\Phi_\lambda(\pi):=\Gamma_\lambda(V_\lambda^\pi,\cdot)\)), i.e., start from some \(y_0\in\mathbb{R}^d\) (or \(\pi_0\)) and generate \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) (or \(\pi_{n+1}=\Phi_\lambda(\pi_n)\)).

Give conditions under which the single-valued fixed-point iteration
\[y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\qquad (n\ge 0)
\]
converges (e.g., from arbitrary initialization, or from some specified set of initial points) to a fixed point \(y^\star\in\mathbb{R}^d\) satisfying \(\Psi_\lambda(y^\star)=y^\star\), equivalently producing a regular relaxed equilibrium \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\) for the entropy-regularized time-inconsistent MDP.
open_problem_quote: Certainly, to make this method fully rigorous, it remains to show the theoretic convergence of the single-valued fixed-point iteration, which is a nontrivial problem in itself and will be left for future research.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can computationally test convergence or non-convergence of the iteration on many randomly generated finite-state instances by explicitly evaluating \(\Psi_\lambda\) and iterating, but the space of instances is large and failure modes may be sensitive to parameter choices and initialization.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate updates (damping, averaging, alternative operators, or sufficient-condition discovery) can be represented as code and scored by an automatic fitness signal such as empirical convergence rate or basin of attraction over a distribution of MDP instances, making evolutionary search plausible for discovering robustly convergent schemes or sharp conjectures.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The open problem in Bayraktar–Huang–Wang–Zhou asks for verifiable conditions ensuring convergence of the naive fixed-point iteration \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\), where \(\Psi_\lambda(y)=V^{\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)}_\lambda\) and \(\Gamma_\lambda\) is the Gibbs (softmax) best-response map induced by entropy regularization. In the source paper, existence of a fixed point is obtained via Brouwer by showing \(\Psi_\lambda\) is continuous and maps a large ball to itself, using the key entropy growth estimate \(\sup_i |H(\Gamma_\lambda(y,i))|\lesssim \ln(1+|y|)\). However, no contraction or monotonicity property is established, so convergence of the straightforward iteration remains unresolved in general.

The closest partial progress comes from Huang–Wang–Zhou (2022), which proves convergence of policy iteration / soft policy updates for entropy-regularized stochastic control problems. Although their setting is time-consistent control rather than time-inconsistent equilibrium, the analytic ingredients overlap strongly: Gibbs-form improvement operators, entropy bounds, and continuity/compactness arguments. Their results suggest that convergence can be proved when one can strengthen the source paper’s continuity/self-mapping property to a contraction (e.g., under sufficiently strong discounting, small Lipschitz constants of \(u\mapsto p_i^u\) and \(u\mapsto f(0,i,u)\), or small enough effective horizon), or when \(\Psi_\lambda\) is order-preserving and admits a unique fixed point.

At present, without additional structural assumptions (such as a global contraction of \(\Psi_\lambda\) in a suitable norm, uniqueness of the regular relaxed equilibrium, or a monotone/concave structure enabling global convergence of best-response dynamics), the convergence of \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) from arbitrary initialization appears to remain open. Promising directions include: (i) deriving explicit Lipschitz constants for \(y\mapsto \Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)\) via log-sum-exp smoothing and combining them with sensitivity bounds for \(\pi\mapsto V^\pi_\lambda\); (ii) proving \(\Psi_\lambda\) is a contraction for large \(\lambda\) (strong regularization) or for sufficiently small \(\|p_i^{u_1}-p_i^{u_2}\|\) (weak control influence), then using continuation in \(\lambda\); and (iii) studying damped iterations \(y_{n+1}=(1-\eta)y_n+\eta\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) where \(\eta\) is chosen to enforce contraction.

sources.title: Convergence of policy iteration for entropy-regularized stochastic control problems
sources.theorem_or_result: Proves convergence of policy iteration / soft policy updates for entropy-regularized stochastic control under conditions yielding a contraction/monotonicity structure; provides entropy estimates and continuity arguments that control the Gibbs (softmax) operator.
sources.connection: Directly addresses convergence of iterative schemes for entropy-regularized control with Gibbs-form updates, supplying techniques (entropy bounds, continuity, contraction-type arguments) that can be adapted to the equilibrium operator \(\Psi_\lambda\) / \(\Phi_\lambda\) in the time-inconsistent setting.

sources.title: Taming the noise in reinforcement learning via soft updates
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces soft (entropy-regularized) policy updates and analyzes stability properties of soft updates in RL, motivating Gibbs/softmax operators as smooth best responses.
sources.connection: Provides background on Gibbs-form policy operators and iterative soft updates; while not about time inconsistency, it motivates fixed-point iterations for softmax operators akin to \(\Phi_\lambda\).

sources.title: Reinforcement learning in continuous time and space: A stochastic control approach
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops entropy-regularized control and RL connections in continuous time, including softmax/Gibbs characterizations and iterative schemes for control.
sources.connection: Background on entropy-regularized control and Gibbs operators; may offer analytical tools (e.g., regularity, contraction under discounting) relevant when adapting convergence proofs to \(\Psi_\lambda\).

sources.title: Maximum entropy inverse reinforcement learning
sources.theorem_or_result: Establishes maximum-entropy principle leading to Gibbs distributions over trajectories/policies; foundational for entropy regularization and softmax policies.
sources.connection: Foundational background for the Gibbs-form operator \(\Gamma_\lambda\) used in the fixed-point map \(\Psi_\lambda\).

sources.title: Time-inconsistent optimal control problems and the equilibrium HJB equation

sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces equilibrium HJB framework for time-inconsistent control, providing analytical characterization of subgame-perfect equilibria.
sources.connection: Background on equilibrium concepts and fixed-point characterizations in time-inconsistent control; informs how \(\Psi_\lambda\) arises as an equilibrium operator though not about convergence of its iteration.
